articles.title: States’ anti-monopoly case against Live Nation continues Monday
articles.description: The Live Nation-Ticketmaster trial is back on. Dozens of states are expected to move forward with their claims against the company's alleged concert industry monopoly beginning on Monday, following a brief hearing on Friday. The Justice Department and a handful of states have accepted settlements with the company, but the majority of the 40 state […]
articles.author: Lauren Feiner

articles.title: Spotify tests letting users directly customize their Taste Profile
articles.description: Spotify Premium users in New Zealand will be the first to experience the service's latest personalization feature. The company is letting users view and make changes to their Taste Profile directly. If your algorithm is serving up too much Bieber, you can politely tell the algorithm to ease up. Beta testers will be able to […]
articles.author: Terrence O’Brien

articles.title: Microsoft’s Copilot AI assistant is coming to current-gen Xbox consoles this year
articles.description: Xbox is getting ready to launch its Gaming Copilot AI assistant on "current-generation consoles" this year, according to a report from GamesRadar. Sonali Yadav, Xbox's product manager for gaming AI, revealed the news during a panel at the Game Developers Conference (GDC), adding that the company will also add the assistant to "more services that […]
articles.author: Emma Roth

articles.title: Trump Mobile is just one in the crowd of conservative carriers
articles.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. This week, I wanted to see how Trump Mobile stacks up to its conservative carrier competition. Trump Mobile isn't unique. I mean, it is in some pretty specific senses - it's not every day the president's family launches a phone company while […]
articles.author: Dominic Preston

articles.title: Digg’s open beta shuts down after just two months, blaming AI bot spam
articles.description: It's only been a year since Digg founder Kevin Rose, Reddit cofounder Alexis Ohanian, and a few others announced the link-sharing site would relaunch, promising a "social discovery built by communities, not by algorithms." Now, two months after opening its Reddit-like platform to the public, Digg is announcing a "hard reset" that's shutting down operations […]
articles.author: Richard Lawler

articles.title: Instagram is getting rid of end-to-end encrypted DMs that ‘very few’ people used
articles.description: Instagram will no longer support end-to-end encrypted messages starting May 8th. In a statement to The Verge, Meta spokesperson Dina El-Kassaby Luce says the platform is discontinuing the feature because "very few people" were using E2EE in their DMs. Instagram has begun notifying impacted users about the change inside its app, and has also posted […]
articles.author: Emma Roth

articles.title: Google Pixel 10A review: Just buy the 9A
articles.description: I'm not entirely sure why the Pixel 10A exists. Google hasn't upgraded the chipset, cameras, or battery in the new phone, and the tweaks it's made elsewhere are minimal at best. The flatter camera island is good, I guess! In one sense this isn't a big problem: The Pixel 9A is an excellent device, and […]
articles.author: Dominic Preston

articles.title: Backbone’s versatile pro controller is nearly matching its best price to date
articles.description: Mobile gaming has come a long way over the course of the last decade or so, but we all know that smartphones simply can’t match the visceral, tactile feel you get while playing with a dedicated controller. Luckily, Backbone makes some excellent mobile options — including last year’s Backbone Pro, which is on sale at […]
articles.author: Brandon Widder

articles.title: Adobe will pay $75 million to settle US cancellation fee lawsuit
articles.description: Adobe says it will pay $75 million to resolve a lawsuit filed by the US government alleging that the creative software giant harmed consumers by making its subscriptions intentionally hard to cancel and concealing termination fees. The payment aims to resolve the complaint raised in June 2024, in which the US Justice Department accused Adobe […]
articles.author: Jess Weatherbed

articles.title: Ninja’s star Creami ice cream maker is on sale for $169
articles.description: If you’ve ever wanted more control over what goes into your ice cream, the Ninja Creami 5‑in‑1 Ice Cream Maker makes it easy. The ice cream maker lets you whip up dessert exactly as you want it from the comfort of your home, and right now it’s on sale for $169 ($30 off) at Walmart.  […]
articles.author: Sheena Vasani
